etymology.description: 日(日) (rì, 'sun; day') as semantic + 寺(寺) as phonetic -> associated with time; ancient form 旹 with 日 and 之(之) (zhī, 'governing'), referring to the passing of time -> over time, 寸(寸) (cùn, 'inch') added to form current 時
etymology.interpretation: The character 時 (shí, 'time; season') stems from the interplay between 日(日) (rì, 'sun; day'), which signifies the passage of time marked by the sun, and 寺(寺) as the phonetic component capturing the sound. The historical forms show an evolution from 旹, merging 日 and 之(之) (zhī, 'governing') to imply governance over time, to its modern form, where 寸(寸) ('inch') likely emphasizes precision or measurement in the concept of time.
etymology.simplification: In 时 (shí), the component 寺(寺) was retained for phonetic purposes, while 日(日) (rì, 'sun; day') was simplified to resemble 日 with fewer strokes, streamlining the character.

examples.chinese: 我们相识时(我們相識時)，天已经暗了(天已經暗了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xiāngshí shí, tiān yǐjīng àn le.
examples.english: When we met, the sky was already dark.

examples.chinese: 在这样的时刻(在這樣的時刻)，我们需要团结(我們需要團結)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhèyàng de shíkè, wǒmen xūyào tuánjié.
examples.english: At such a time, we need to unite.

examples.chinese: 时光流逝(時光流逝)，童年的回忆依然鲜活(童年的回憶依然鮮活)。
examples.pinyin: Shíguāng liúshì, tóngnián de huíyì yīrán xiānhuó.
examples.english: As time passes, childhood memories remain vivid.
